ops.title: athome's ensure_write_auth() KeyErrors on an OAuth HF token — blocks the Modal/Tinker paid push paths; the only write credential on this machine is OAuth
ops.body:
`athome.hf.ensure_write_auth()` reads `info["auth"]["accessToken"]["role"]`, which **KeyErrors on an OAuth token** whose whoami is `auth == {"type": "oauth", ...}` (no `accessToken`). It's called at `athome/train/modal.py` in `ModalTrainBackend.train()` *before* the SpendGuard and `app.run()`, so the Modal paid path crashes deterministically at **zero GPU cost** — and the same preflight gates the HF push in the Tinker path.

This blocked the v0.4.0 live Modal LoRA-SFT paid smoke (2026-07-14): the only write-capable HF credential on this machine is the OAuth token; the classic `yasyf-home` token is role-`read` (see [[hf-token-yasyf-home-read-only]]). The OAuth token itself genuinely writes (proven by a create+push+delete round-trip) — the bug is purely the whoami-shape assumption, not the credential.

**Why:** it rejects the token minted by the standard `huggingface-cli login` OAuth flow, so any OAuth-authed user hits it on every Modal/Tinker run — a real release blocker, not just a smoke-env quirk.

**How to apply:** the fix is to branch on `auth["type"]` in `ensure_write_auth` (`"oauth"` ⇒ write-capable; `"access_token"` ⇒ require `accessToken.role == "write"`) with a regression test. Until then, unblock a paid smoke by exposing a classic/fine-grained HF **write** token as `HF_TOKEN` (athome's `HfSettings.token` reads that env var via validation_alias; it is NOT auto-populated from `~/.cache/huggingface/token`). Everything else in the Modal smoke preflighted green (`ModalTrainBackend.available()`, base `qwen3-8b` `serves_locally=True`, local `datasets 5.0.0` matches the pin, projected H100 bill ≈ $0.33 for steps=2/batch=1/seq=256).
